data.title: Alpha and Omega to pay $4.25M to settle with US over export control practices
data.description:
Alpha and Omega Semiconductor (NASDAQ:AOSL) agreed to pay $4.25M to settle with the U.S. Department of Commerce for allegedly shipping items to China's Huawei Technologies in violation of export rules.

Alpha said it reached a resolution with the department's Bureau of Industry and Security, or BIS, to close its investigation of the company's export control practices.

The company noted that the resolution does not impact its ongoing business operations and brings to an end the U.S. government’s five-year-plus investigation, which resulted in no criminal charges.

Alpha engaged in prohibited conduct by forwarding 1,650 power controllers, smart power stages and related accessories to Huawei without authorization in 2019, Reuters reported, citing a department order. Huawei was added to a restricted U.S. trade list in 2019.

The U.S. Department of Commerce did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

Alpha said on Wednesday that it is pleased to bring this matter to a close with only limited administrative export control charges.

data.title: Robinhood ticks lower as OpenAI rejects tokenized equity tokens
data.description:
OpenAI issued a firm statement Wednesday denying any affiliation with the tokenized equity tokens offered by trading platform Robinhood, emphasizing that the tokens do not represent real ownership or equity in the company.

The trading platform began offering tokenized shares of OpenAI and SpaceX (SPACE) to its users in Europe.

“These ‘OpenAI tokens’ are not OpenAI equity,” OpenAI wrote on X. “We did not partner with Robinhood, were not involved in this, and do not endorse it.”

The company said that “any transfer of OpenAI equity requires our approval — we did not approve any transfer,” and warned users to “please be careful.”

Robinhood down 1.5% in premarket trading.

 

 

The news follows Robinhood's (NASDAQ:HOOD) introduction of a suite of new crypto-focused products, from tokenized equities and exchange-traded funds to crypto perpetual futures to crypto staking.

“These tokens give retail investors indirect exposure to private markets, opening up access, and are enabled by Robinhood’s ownership stake in a special purpose vehicle,” a Robinhood spokesperson said in response to the OpenAI post.

data.title: Ford CEO: AI will replace half of all white-collar workers in U.S.
data.description:
"Artificial intelligence is going to replace literally half of all white-collar workers in the U.S.," Ford (NYSE:F) CEO Jim Farley predicted at a recent event, the latest company chief to forecast the impact of the rapidly evolving technology on the workforce.

Farley believes that AI and new technologies have an asymmetric impact on the economy. "… that means a lot of things are helped a lot and a lot of things are hurt. AI will leave a lot of white-collar people behind."

Other top executives have made similar predictions in recent months. Amazon (AMZN) CEO Andy Jassy told employees that efficiency gains from AI would likely reduce the company's workforce in the next few years.

Dario Amodei, CEO of Amazon (AMZN)-backed AI startup Anthropic, told Axios that AI could wipe out half of all entry-level white-collar jobs and spike unemployment to 10%-20% in the next 1-5 years.

Marianne Lake, head of JPMorgan's (JPM) consumer and community banking unit, said operations headcount could decline about 10% in the coming years amid growing AI adoption.

Meta (META) CEO Mark Zuckerberg in April predicted that most of the company's code could be written by AI in the next 12-18 months.

"AI is coming for your jobs," Micha Kaufman, CEO of freelance services marketplace Fiverr (FVRR) warned in April. "It does not matter if you are a programmer, designer, product manager, data scientist, lawyer, customer support rep, salesperson, or a finance person - AI is coming for you."

data.title: Mkango and Crown PropTech merge to create strategic global rare earths platform
data.description:
Mkango Resources (OTCPK:MKNGF) announced that its wholly-owned subsidiary, Lancaster Exploration Limited (soon to be renamed Mkango Rare Earths Limited, and together with other Mkango subsidiaries, the "MKAR Group"), has signed a definitive business combination agreement with Crown PropTech Acquisitions (OTC:CPTKW), for a pro forma valuation of US$400 million for Mkango's stake in MKAR. 

This proposed merger aims to create a new publicly traded, focused solely on rare earths. The new entity, to be named Mkango Rare Earths Limited, will combine the Songwe Hill and Pulawy projects, and its ordinary shares are anticipated to trade on Nasdaq.

The valuation does not include MKAR's debt, closing cash, transaction expenses, or any net proceeds from a PIPE financing and CPTK's trust account.

The move will support Mkango Rare Earths Limited's strategic growth plan, which includes development of Songwe Hill and Pulawy.

MKAR's sponsors agreed to a US$500,000 funding and an additional US$250,000 for convertible promissory notes to support expenses for a proposed business combination, pending regulatory approval.

Pursuant to the Business Combination Agreement, MKAR will split shares, resulting in 40 million shares for Mkango and 6.9 million for CPTK shareholders, with convertible notes converting into shares or cash based on specific terms.

Furthermore, outstanding warrants of CPTK include about 9.2 million public warrants and 5.0 million private placement warrants, which will be exercisable for ordinary shares of MKAR as part of the Proposed Business Combination. The public warrants have a five-year term, a cash exercise price of $11.50 per share, and can be redeemed for $0.01 per warrant with a 30-day notice if certain stock price conditions are met.

The deal is expected to be completed in the fourth quarter of 2025.

The net proceeds from the proposed business combination are expected to support MKAR Group's strategic growth plan, which includes development of Songwe Hill and Pulawy.

data.title: Germany's composite PMI hits 3-month high in June
data.description:
• Composite PMI in Germany increased to 50.40 points in June, a 3-month high, from 48.50 points in May.

  • Services PMI in Germany increased to 49.70 points in June from 47.10 points in May of 2025.

data.title: AirAsia close to agreement on long-range Airbus narrowbody jet – Bloomberg
data.description:
AirAsia (OTC:AIABF) is nearing an agreement to buy dozens more Airbus SE (OTCPK:EADSF) single-aisle aircraft, in a deal that could be announced during Malaysian Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim’s state visit to France this week, Bloomberg reported, citing people familiar with the matter.

The Southeast Asian budget airline is in talks with the European planemaker—its sole aircraft supplier—about expanding its aircraft order, according to people familiar with the matter who requested anonymity due to the private nature of the discussions.

The advanced discussions for extra-long-range narrowbody aircraft could still end up with no deal, they said.

The budget carrier has also been discussing an order for up to 100 of Airbus’ smaller A220 jets, the report said.
